title: Spec ID Format
description: Without a single canonical format for referencing specs, workspace prefixes and capability path segments become ambiguous — different subsystems would parse the same string differently. This spec defines the canonical spec ID format, parsing rules, and storage conventions, ensuring every spec is identified unambiguously as a `workspace:capabilityPath` string across all of specd.
context: Without a single canonical format for referencing specs, workspace prefixes and capability path segments become ambiguous — different subsystems would parse the same string differently. This spec defines the canonical spec ID format, parsing rules, and storage conventions, ensuring every spec is identified unambiguously as a `workspace:capabilityPath` string across all of specd.

rules:
The canonical spec ID format SHALL be `workspace:capabilityPath`, where:

- `workspace` is the workspace name (e.g. `default`, `billing`)
- `capabilityPath` is the slash-separated path to the spec within the workspace (e.g. `auth/login`, `_global/architecture`)

The workspace and capability path are separated by `:` (colon). All `/` characters belong to the capability path.

This format is used everywhere: internal storage (`change.specIds`, manifest), domain-layer references, CLI input, metadata `dependsOn`, and user-facing output. There is a single format — no internal vs external distinction.

When no `:` is present, the spec ID SHALL be interpreted as a capability path in the `default` workspace.

For example, `auth/login` with no colon resolves to workspace `default`, capability path `auth/login`. Its fully-qualified form is `default:auth/login`.
When a colon-qualified spec ID specifies a workspace name that does not exist in the project configuration, the parser SHALL reject the input with an error.

Parsing a spec ID string SHALL follow these steps:

1. Find the first `:` in the string.
2. If found: everything before `:` is the workspace, everything after is the capability path. Validate the workspace exists.
3. If not found: workspace is `default`, the entire string is the capability path.

There is no slash-based workspace detection. The `/` character is never used as a workspace separator.
Functions that accept spec IDs SHOULD normalize bare paths to fully-qualified form (`default:capPath`) at the system boundary. Domain-layer code MAY assume spec IDs are already qualified.

constraints: Workspace names MUST NOT contain / or : characters
Capability paths MUST NOT contain : characters
The separator is always : (colon) — / is never a workspace separator
The default workspace is implicit when no colon is present

scenarios.name: Simple spec ID with default workspace
then: workspace is default and capability path is auth/login

scenarios.name: Non-default workspace
then: workspace is billing and capability path is invoices/create

scenarios.name: Multi-segment capability path
then: workspace is default and capability path is common/conventions

scenarios.name: Bare path defaults to default workspace
then: workspace is default and capability path is auth/login

scenarios.name: Single-segment bare path
then: workspace is default and capability path is architecture

scenarios.name: Colon syntax with unknown workspace
given: nonexistent is not a configured workspace
then: an error is raised indicating the workspace is unknown

scenarios.name: Colon takes precedence over slash
then: workspace is billing and capability path is auth/login
AND the / in auth/login is never interpreted as a workspace separator

scenarios.name: Slash is never a workspace separator
then: workspace is default and capability path is billing/auth/login

scenarios.name: specDependsOn uses canonical format
given: a spec at default:auth/login with dependsOn: ['auth/session']
then: specDependsOn values contain default:auth/session in fully-qualified format

scenarios.name: Cross-workspace specDependsOn
given: a spec at billing:invoices/create with dependsOn: ['billing:invoices/shared']
then: specDependsOn contains billing:invoices/shared and traversal uses workspace billing

scenarios.name: Bare path normalized to fully-qualified
then: it is normalized to default:auth/login before reaching the domain layer
